title: cochlearia armoracia var. macrocarpa
intro: Cochlearia armoracia var. macrocarpa, also known as horseradish, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and act as a natural antibacterial agent. The health benefits of horseradish include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and aiding in the treatment of sinus congestion and sore throats. Horseradish can be prepared as a root extract, oil, or supplement, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine, including as a topical treatment for rheumatism and skin conditions.
